title: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises TD Garden environment amid trade rumors
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Celtics fans and TD Garden's passionate environment during a recent livestream amid trade rumors.
Boston and Miami Heat are frontrunners to land Antetokounmpo, but Miami is currently considered the favorite.
Speculation continues on whether Jaylen Brown might be part of a trade package for Antetokounmpo to the Celtics.

title: Harvard ‘Senior Sunrise’ cut short by cops
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police cleared Harvard's annual Senior Sunrise on Weeks Footbridge, citing crowd concerns and attempted swimming in Charles River.
Massachusetts State Police, with Cambridge and Harvard Police, responded shortly after 5 a.m. to reports of a large crowd.
Senior Sunrise gathering follows Harvard's 2026 commencement where Conan O'Brien was the keynote speaker.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair' is now available for streaming on Peacock.
'The Four Seasons' Season 2 starring Tina Fey and Steve Carell is streaming on Netflix.
'Spider-Noir' featuring Nicolas Cage is streaming on Prime Video in color or black & white.

title: Red Sox predicted to draft 18-year-old third baseman in 2026 MLB Draft by ESPN
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: ESPN's Kiley McDaniel predicts the Red Sox will draft Bo Lowrance, a third baseman from South Carolina, in 2026.
Lowrance is an 18-year-old, 6-foot-5 player with a left-handed bat, compared to Freddie Freeman by some scouts.
The projected first-round pick for Boston is committed to the University of Virginia but might switch positions from third base.

title: Lowell father and daughter charged in alleged payroll tax scheme
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lim Ou, 69, and Nalen Ou, 39, charged with federal tax crimes for alleged payroll tax evasion.
Authorities claim the duo concealed over $48 million and failed to pay $12 million in taxes.
If convicted, both face up to five years in prison and substantial fines and restitution.

title: Report: Christian Gonzalez unlikely to participate in OTAs under current Patriots contract
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Christian Gonzalez is expected to skip Patriots OTAs due to contract extension negotiations.
Gonzalez aims for a contract similar to Trent McDuffie's $124 million deal.
Patriots cleared cap space by restructuring Mike Onwenu's contract, potentially aiding in Gonzalez's extension.

title: Search underway for driver who led cops on a chase through Everett, Malden, Stoneham
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A suspect led police on a chase through Everett, Malden, and Stoneham, striking a State Police cruiser.
The driver, wanted on drug warrants, abandoned the vehicle and fled on foot with a passenger in Stoneham.
No injuries were reported, and police advise the public there's no ongoing threat as the search continues.

title: Ex-Patriots HC Jerod Mayo has a new job away from football
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jerod Mayo, former Patriots head coach, is now managing director at Needham-based private equity firm Fifth Down Capital.
Mayo, 40, joined Fifth Down Capital in February, transitioning from a previous role at Optum as VP of Business Development.
Mayo was fired after the 2024 Patriots season, completing it with a 4-13 record and two six-game losing streaks.

title: Mass. man reports beating Mount Everest speed climbing record
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Massachusetts native Tyler Andrews summited Mount Everest in 9 hours 55 minutes, beating a 23-year-old speed climbing record.
Andrews's GPS tracking shows he surpassed Nepali climber Lhakpa Gelu Sherpa's 2003 record of 10 hours 56 minutes; authorities to verify.
Despite overcoming challenges like bad weather, Andrews dedicated his record climb to late friend Greg Krupa, a fellow runner.

title: You may complain about the T, but a new ranking says it’s one of the best in the U.S.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Travel + Leisure ranked Boston third for public transportation in the U.S., praised for extensive transit options.
Boston's MBTA offers efficient connections with a bus network reaching inaccessible train areas, facilitating car-free living.
The MBTA's Commuter Rail facilitates travel to destinations outside Boston, offers promotions for major events.
